vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: Standard configuration change to /etc/systemd/logind.conf that terminates idle sessions; does not affect authentication mechanisms or critical services and follows STIG hardening patterns.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; May terminate legitimate long‑running sessions if timeout is set too low

review_feedback: Restarting systemd-logind during an active session can tear down existing login and PAM sessions, potentially dropping the administrator’s SSH session or other interactive services. While the configuration change itself is correct, the action of “systemctl restart systemd-logind.service” introduces a risk of brief service interruption or lockout that is not covered by the remediation logic.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly identifies and modifies the StopIdleSessionSec setting in /etc/systemd/logind.conf, and restarts systemd-logind to apply changes. This will terminate idle sessions after 5 minutes, fulfilling the vulnerability requirement. The approach is functionally adequate but could be refined for robustness.
concerns: No backup of the original configuration file is performed, which may hinder rollback if needed.
The plan uses a single sed command without verifying that the file is in a valid state before writing, potentially corrupting the file if multiple [Login] sections exist.
Systemd may cache the configuration; a daemon-reload might be preferred over a full restart to preserve other active sessions.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf before making changes (e.g., cp logind.conf{,.bak}) to allow rollback.
Use a more robust script that verifies the presence of a single [Login] section and inserts or updates the setting with a clear, idempotent sed or awk command.

After modifying the file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' followed by 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' only if the file was actually changed, or use 'systemctl restart systemd-logind.service' unconditionally as done here, which is acceptable for most environments.
Add a check for file permissions and ownership before attempting the sed operation to avoid permission errors.
